articles.title: Easter
articles.author: Grace Ingles Frost
articles.complete_text:
Easter
By Grace Ingles Frost

The bonds were riven,
And the angels twain
Stood in the crypt
Where for three days had lain
The corpse of Him
Who suffered, on the cross,
That all mankind might be
Freed from the curse
Which bound humanity;
Stood, and the tidings gave—
"He is risen."

The enthralling bars
Of death's cold sombre prison,
Had sundered been
By stainless sacrifice.
The bonds are riven,
And, the angels twain,
One, who is Hope,
The other, who is Love,
Down from the courts,
Where reigns the heavenly King,
Again and yet again
Return to bring
Glad tidings
Unto all created things;
But only life attuned
May hark and hear
The message voiced by them,
From year to year.

The bonds are riven,
And with time's recurring hour,
When speak the angels,
Every leaf and flower
Comes forth rejuvenate
From lethargy
Of winter's dreamless sleep;
The birds make blither music:
And the breeze
More gladsome sings
Among the verdant trees.
All nature
With the ecstasy is rife.
But what of man?
He of the higher life:
Sees he each year
The miracle renew,
And unto it responds
As God would, have him do?
articles.notes: This is a complete Easter poem by Grace Ingles Frost that appears in the magazine content and is not listed among the existing extracted articles. It's substantial standalone literary content, not misc material.

misc_summary: The remaining content consists of the magazine's table of contents, publication information, subscription details, advertisements for sacrament sets, temple garments, ice cream/candy, and various other commercial products, plus a brief caption about women at the Tomb of the Kings.
